articles.title: Utah’s anti-gambling tradition meets Kalshi and Polymarket in a new legal fight
articles.summary: Utah's strict anti-gambling laws are colliding with prediction markets like Kalshi and Polymarket, setting up a legal battle that could determine whether these platforms can operate in the state. The outcome will test whether prediction markets can successfully argue they're skill-based information tools rather than gambling, potentially creating precedent for other restrictive states.

articles.title: Prime Brokers Race to Give Wall Street Access to Event Bets
articles.summary: Prime brokers are scrambling to offer their Wall Street clients access to prediction markets and event-driven betting platforms. This institutional rush validates prediction markets as a legitimate asset class and could pump serious liquidity into political, economic, and cultural event betting.

articles.title: Tesla Loses Director Behind Robotaxi Backend: What Do Prediction Markets Say?
articles.summary: Tesla's director responsible for the robotaxi backend infrastructure has departed the company, creating another leadership gap in their autonomous vehicle division. Prediction markets tracking Tesla's robotaxi timeline and Full Self-Driving milestones will probably see increased volatility as investors reassess whether the company can hit its ambitious 2024-2025 deployment targets without key technical leadership.

articles.title: Betting markets have a new favorite for the 2028 presidential election
articles.summary: Prediction markets are already pricing in a new frontrunner for the 2028 presidential race, signaling early money is flowing toward candidates four years out from the election. This shift reveals how quickly betting markets pivot after major political events, offering bettors a chance to lock in odds before the field gets crowded and prices tighten.

articles.title: Kalshi Still Needs Sports, Even as Courts Close In
articles.summary: Kalshi faces mounting legal pressure but sports betting remains crucial to their business model and growth strategy. Without sports markets driving volume and user engagement, the platform risks losing competitive ground to traditional sportsbooks that are expanding into political and event betting.

articles.title: Sen. Blumenthal proposes bill establishing guardrails for prediction markets
articles.summary: Sen. Richard Blumenthal is pushing legislation to regulate prediction markets with new federal oversight and restrictions. The bill threatens to kneecap the industry's recent growth by potentially limiting what Americans can bet on and how much they can wager.

articles.title: Are Prediction Markets Actually Good for Journalism?
articles.summary: Journalists and media critics are debating whether prediction markets help or hurt news reporting, as betting platforms increasingly influence how stories get covered and what gets attention. The outcome could determine whether newsrooms embrace prediction markets as reporting tools or dismiss them as gambling distractions that skew editorial priorities toward whatever drives the most action.

articles.title: Polymarket’s Fight Is for Next Generation of Financial Markets
articles.summary: Polymarket is battling regulators and traditional finance incumbents to establish prediction markets as a legitimate asset class for mainstream investors. If they win, we're looking at billions in institutional capital flowing into markets that currently operate on the fringes with retail crypto money.

articles.title: Koch Q&A on KY Fixed-Odds Bill: Predictive Markets 'Absolutely Cannibalizing' Other Gambling
articles.summary: A Koch Industries executive told Kentucky lawmakers that prediction markets are eating into traditional gambling revenues as the state considers legalizing fixed-odds sports betting. The admission signals that prediction markets have grown large enough to threaten established gambling interests, which could either accelerate regulatory crackdowns or legitimize the space as serious competition.

articles.title: Will the SAVE Act Pass? See Latest Odds from Prediction Markets
articles.summary: Prediction markets are pricing the odds that Congress will pass the SAVE Act, legislation requiring proof of citizenship to vote in federal elections. The bill's fate hinges on Republican unity and Democratic resistance, making it a pure partisan play that bettors can trade on partisan sentiment and legislative momentum.

articles.title: Top US exchange executives call for clearer rules as prediction markets grow
articles.summary: US exchange executives are pushing regulators for clearer rules as prediction markets gain mainstream traction. The lobbying signals that major players see regulatory clarity as the key to unlocking institutional money and scaling beyond the current retail-focused market.

articles.title: Polymarket Taps Palantir, TWG AI to Police Growing Sports Bets
articles.summary: Polymarket signed deals with Palantir and TWG AI to monitor and detect suspicious activity across its expanding sports betting markets. The move signals serious investment in fraud prevention as sports betting drives massive volume growth, but also suggests regulatory pressure is forcing prediction markets to adopt traditional sportsbook-style surveillance infrastructure.
